steps.text: Clarify the scope: define early-onset Alzheimer’s disease (EOAD), how it differs from late-onset AD, and what “promising treatments currently being researched” should include (drug classes, non-drug interventions, trial phases).

Identify major categories of disease-modifying pharmacologic treatments under active research for EOAD (e.g., anti-amyloid, anti-tau, neuroinflammation, synaptic/neuronal protection, gene-targeted therapies) and key representative agents in each.

Investigate symptomatic and non-pharmacologic interventions being researched specifically for EOAD (e.g., cognitive training, neuromodulation, lifestyle/precision medicine approaches, psychosocial interventions for younger patients).

Assess current clinical trial evidence and development stage for the most promising EOAD-relevant treatments (efficacy signals, safety concerns, regulatory status, and whether trials include or target EOAD).
Synthesize which approaches appear most promising for EOAD specifically, highlight key ongoing trials, and outline realistic near- and medium-term prospects and uncertainties.
